timeline.label: Q2 FY2026: H1 revenue $13.02B (+12.3% YoY); diluted EPS $8.86 H1 — growth re-accelerating
FY2025 10-K: revenue $23.77B, FCF $9.85B, RPO $22.52B, $11.28B share repurchases
$2.0B senior notes offering (2028/2030/2035 tranches) to fund returns and general corporate purposes
FY2024 10-K filed: revenue $21.51B; operating income recovery continues post-2023 restructuring
Terminated Figma acquisition after regulatory opposition; paid $1.0B break fee — design collaboration remains competitive gap
Firefly generative AI commercially embedded across Creative Cloud — AI product cycle begins
Announced Figma acquisition ($20B) — later terminated; signaled push into collaborative design
Completed shift to subscription-only Creative Cloud — recurring revenue model fully in place
Shantanu Narayen CEO (since 2007) — long-tenured leadership through cloud transition
Acquired Omniture — foundation of Digital Experience Cloud enterprise marketing stack
Acquired Macromedia (Flash, Dreamweaver) — expanded creative suite breadth
PDF format standardized via Acrobat — document franchise established

industry.structure: Creative and marketing software is oligopolistic at the professional tier (Adobe dominant in design/PDF) with fragmented challengers in prosumer and generative-AI niches. Enterprise marketing clouds compete with Salesforce, Google, and Oracle.
industry.share_shift: Adobe is holding professional creative share while low-cost tools (Canva, AI image generators) attack individual and SMB tiers; Experience Cloud competes for enterprise marketing budgets with mixed win rates.
industry.trend: Generative AI is embedding into creative workflows; subscription RPO and AI credit monetization are reshaping pricing; regulatory scrutiny blocked the Figma deal, leaving collaborative design as an open competitive front.
